patch.value: Every open npm Dependabot PR fails Validate (Bun) at bun install --frozen-lockfile because bun.lock is left stale. GitHub now officially supports package-ecosystem bun for text bun.lock files, so the repository should use its declared package manager end to end.
Dependabot updates do not maintain bun.lock
The npm ecosystem updater creates package-lock.json changes while the required Bun frozen install consumes bun.lock.
Dependabot uses Bun ecosystem support; dependency PRs update bun.lock; CI does not create or rely on package-lock.json
Failure log and official ecosystem support are confirmed.
Open any npm Dependabot PR and inspect Validate (Bun): lockfile had changes but lockfile is frozen.
Automated dependency PRs update the repository's authoritative Bun lockfile.
Dependabot emits npm lock state and leaves bun.lock stale.
Security and maintenance updates remain unmergeable.

value.author: codex-root
value.text: Switch .github/dependabot.yml from npm to bun; do not add package-lock.json to a Bun-owned repository.

value.author: codex-root
value.text: Dependabot Bun support requires the modern text bun.lock, which this repo already uses.

author: codex-root

patch.value: Native Bun PRs #48 and #49 updated bun.lock and executed Bun validation; scoped ignores now prevent known-incompatible majors from reopening.
Automated dependency PRs update bun.lock and pass frozen Bun installation.
Changed Dependabot to package-ecosystem bun, retained bun.lock as authoritative, grouped compatible updates, and added explicit ignores for unsupported compiler/runtime type majors.
Native Bun dependency automation delivered in PR #45

message: Close Bun automation issue with live PR proof

author: codex-auto-release

patch.value: Fixed Dependabot updates so bun.lock is maintained
